learning_outcomes.description: Recognise the influence of culture on communication between individuals, groups, organisations, and nations.
Critically reflect on the influence of culture on how you view your own cultural group and cultural groups other than your own.
Effectively apply verbal and non-verbal communication knowledge and skills in intercultural communication contexts.
Effectively apply theories and concepts in analysing intercultural communication problems and provide solutions.
Demonstrate respect, expert judgment, adaptability, and responsibility as an effective and ethical communicator in diverse cultural contexts.

assessments.task_description:
This assessment requires you to examine and reflect on your own culture as an insider. This assessment is not simply a general description of your culture. Your reflective essay should begin with your cultural identification to convey who you are as a member of the cultural group with which you identify. You are required then to explain what beliefs, values, practices, traditions and so forth are important to you as a member of your cultural group, and why they are important to you. Your cultural group can be defined at the national level (e.g., Australia, Germany, Japan) or at sub-cultural levels (e.g., ethnicity, religion, geographic region). You need to apply theories and concepts of communication and culture learned in the course. You may also search in the literature for other relevant theories beyond what is covered in the course and apply them in your reflective essay where appropriate. You must define all key concepts which you have included in your reflective essay and demonstrate your ability to apply them in appropriate contexts. Your reflection explains how your culture shapes your thinking (attitudes), doing (behaviours), and being (cultural identity). You are required to cite at least 3 academic references from academic journals or books. Include all references cited within your reflective essay in the references list at the end of your reflective essay. APA 7th edition referencing style is required. The UQ Library website has APA 7th edition referencing style guide.

Include the following sections in your reflective essay:

  • Title of your reflective essay.
  • Brief description of the culture with which you identify and the aspects of culture that you choose to focus on (e.g., values, beliefs, traditions, or customs, etc.).
  • Definition of key concepts that your reflective essay used, explanation of how they are applied in your essay, and justification for why they are important to you as a member of the cultural group with which you identify.
  • Your reflection on how your culture shapes your thinking, doing, and being.
  • References list with all references cited within your reflective essay (in-text citation) arranged according to APA 7th edition referencing style guide.


Note:

  1. This assessment should be submitted via Turnitin. Submission as email attachment will NOT be accepted.
  2. The word limit (1500 words) is suggestive. No penalties will be applied to your assessment based on the sole reason that it is over or under the suggested word limit.
  3. Your assessment is graded based on the marking criteria provided. It is NOT graded with reference to the achievement of other students in the course or your performance in other courses.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Click the TurnItIn submission link in Assessment 1 Related Resources folder on Blackboard to submit Assessment 1 to TurnItIn.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Assessment 2 consists of 5 in-class tests to be conducted in person during tutorial sessions of 5 weeks, starting in Week 5 and ending in Week 9 (inclusive). The duration of each test is about 30 minutes. The in-class tests are identity verified, in person and closed-book tests. Electronic devices, learning materials, readings, and written notes are not permitted during each test. Tutors will provide hard copies of test worksheets to students in the tutorial session of the week in which a test is scheduled. Students submit their completed test worksheets to their tutor at the end of the test time in the tutorial session.

In-class tests are in the format of short-answer questions. The question(s) of each test are based on the learning resources of a specific week including lecture notes and power points, tutorial notes and power points, lecture, tutorial, and required readings. More details of in-class tests will be provided in class sessions and on Blackboard.

Note:

  1. Each completed in-class test worksheet is to be submitted in the tutorial session in which the test is conducted. Submission of a completed in-class test worksheet to the course coordinator or tutor as email attachment will NOT be accepted.
  2. Your assessment is graded based on the marking criteria specified in this course, NOT graded with reference to the achievement of other students or your performance in other courses.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Each completed in-class test worksheet must be submitted in the tutorial session in which the test is conducted.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: No late submission is permitted as this is in-class and in-person assessment. Please refer to the Extension or Deferral availability notes below if you are unable to take an in-class test on the day of your tutorial in a specific week.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
This assessment requires you to apply theories learned in the course and/or other relevant theories that you have searched in the literature to analyse cases in which intercultural communication problems occurred. You need to choose two cases from the list of case studies provided in the Assessment 3 folder on Blackboard course site. You need to analyse the two cases you have chosen to identify the intercultural problems in each case and then provide specific solutions. You should apply theories of intercultural communication learned in the course and/or other relevant theories you have searched in the literature in analysing problems and providing specific solutions to the identified problems. You are required to reflect on your learning about intercultural communication through your analysis of the intercultural communication cases. You need to cite at least 5 academic references from academic books or journals. Include all references cited within your essay in a references list at the end of your assessment. APA 7th edition referencing style is required. The UQ Library website has APA 7th edition referencing style guide.

Include the following sections in your assessment:

  • Title: Provide a specific title for your essay of case studies.
  • Introduction: State briefly the cases that you have chosen and provide a justification for why you have chosen those two cases for your assessment.
  • Analyse the intercultural communication problems in the two case studies you have chosen and provide specific solutions by drawing on theories learned in the course and/or other relevant theories that you have searched in the literature. This demonstrates your knowledge and ability to apply knowledge in appropriate contexts.
  • Reflection: Reflect on what you have learned about intercultural communication from analysing the two cases studies you have chosen.
  • References: All references cited within the essay must be included in the references list that is arranged according to APA 7th edition referencing style guide.


Note:

  1. This assessment should be submitted via Turnitin. Submission as email attachment will NOT be accepted.
  2. The word limit (2000 words) is suggestive. No penalties will be applied to your assessment based on the sole reason that it is over or under the suggested word limit.
  3. Your assessment is graded based on the marking criteria published in the ECP. Your assessment is NOT graded with reference to the achievement of other students in the course or your performance in other courses.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Click the Turnitin submission link in Assessment 3 Related Resources folder on Blackboard to submit Assessment 3 to Turnitin.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
